uk: Святого мученика Лукиліяна і тих, що з ним
saints.en: St. Martyr Lucillian and those with him

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 Октоїха, 3 мч., Сл., І нині: хрестобогород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар мч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. Катизми черг., сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон Октоїха і мч. По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світильний мч., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – мч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – Рим. 87 зач.; 4, 13-25. Єв. – Мт. 23 зач.; 7, 21-23.

troparion_en.text: By your faith, you shone like a radiant star in the dark night of error; / you fought the good fight and slew the crafty enemy, O Lucillian. / Together with venerable Paula and the four martyred children / entreat Christ our God to save our souls.

kontakion_en.text: You attained the dignity of the martyrs of Christ / through the torments that you courageously endured, O Lucillian. / Together with Paula and the four martyred children, / you sing to the Creator: / “Like sheep we are slaughtered for love of You, O Savior.”

matched_oca_saint: Martyr Lucillian and those who suffered with him at Byzantium

oca_saints.name: Martyr Lucillian and those who suffered with him at Byzantium

troparion.text: By your faith, you shone like a radiant star in the dark night of error; / you fought the good fight and slew the crafty enemy, O Lucillian. / Together with venerable Paula and the four martyred children / entreat Christ our God to save our souls.

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Lucian, Bishop of Beauvais, and those with him in France

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

In you, O Father, was preserved with exactness what was according to the image;  /  for you took up the Cross and followed Christ.  /  By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away; / but to care for the soul, since it is immortal.  /  Therefore, O Venerable Kevin, your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
Having divinely armed yourself with purity of soul / and firmly grasping unceasing prayer as a spear, you have slain the armies of the demons.  /  Therefore, we beseech you, O Father Kevin: /  “Always intercede for those who honor you.”
